name: codex-approval-never-absent-deny
doc: Baseline/regression: the neverApproval exemption must NEVER fire without the exact `permission_mode:"bypassPermissions"` signal. Both the total absence of the field and the collapsed `"default"` value (what the other 3 approval policies map to — codex-rs/core/src/hook_runtime.rs) fall straight through to the untouched GIT_ASK -> downgraded-deny path, byte-identical to scenario 23.

steps.doc: No `permission_mode` field at all (pre-0.1.65 payload shape) — event.permissionMode is undefined, isBypassPermissions(undefined) is false, neverApproval stays false. `git commit` hits the normal GIT_ASK, downgraded to deny on Codex.

`permission_mode:"default"` — the value every approval policy OTHER than Never collapses to. Must NOT be mistaken for the exemption signal (fail-closed, no fuzzy/prefix matching) — same deny as step 1.
